---
title: "Protocol 1: Behavioral Baseline"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
    code-fold: true
    code-tools: true
    include-in-header:
      - text: |
          <style>
          .g-col-3 { min-width: 0; }
          .g-col-3 .cell-output-display { overflow-x: hidden; }
          .g-col-3 .mosaic-widget label { flex-direction: column; align-items: stretch; min-height: unset; font-size: 0.85rem; }
          .g-col-3 .ts-wrapper { width: 100% !important; margin-left: 0 !important; margin-right: 0 !important; }
          .g-col-3 .ts-control { min-width: 0 !important; font-size: 0.85rem; }
          </style>
params:
  evals_path: "outputs/viz/default/evals.parquet"
---

```{python}
#| tags: [parameters]
evals_path = "outputs/viz/default/evals.parquet"
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
import sys
from pathlib import Path

import pandas as pd

project_root = Path(".").resolve().parent
if str(project_root) not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, str(project_root))

def _resolve_path(p: str) -> str:
    pp = Path(p)
    if pp.is_absolute() and pp.exists():
        return str(pp)
    candidate = project_root / p
    if candidate.exists():
        return str(candidate)
    candidate = Path(".").resolve() / p
    if candidate.exists():
        return str(candidate)
    return p

evals_path_resolved = _resolve_path(evals_path)
_evals_df = pd.read_parquet(evals_path_resolved)
_out_dir = Path(evals_path_resolved).parent

# N values are strings (converted in prepare_viz_data); sort numerically, keep as strings
n_domain = sorted(_evals_df["n_turns"].unique(), key=int)
max_n = n_domain[-1]

# --- Derived datasets ---

# Label data: only rows at max n_turns (for text labels at rightmost point)
_label_df = _evals_df[_evals_df["n_turns"] == max_n].copy()
_label_path = str(_out_dir / "_labels.parquet")
_label_df.to_parquet(_label_path, index=False)

from inspect_viz import Data, Selection
from inspect_viz.input import select

from scripts.viz_helpers import labeled_line_chart, bullet_graph, paired_bullet_graph, overview_heatmap
from scripts.prepare_viz_data import ALIGNMENT_AXIS_PAIRS

# --- Summary statistics: static vs dynamic conditions ---

STATIC_CONDITIONS = {
    "neutral", "value_aligned_cats", "value_misaligned_cats",
    "factual_aligned_earth", "factual_misaligned_earth",
    "token_countries_states", "token_states_countries",
}
_static_mask = _evals_df["condition"].isin(STATIC_CONDITIONS)
_has_static = _static_mask.any()
_has_dynamic = (~_static_mask).any()
_summary_cols = {}
if _has_static:
    _summary_cols["Static avg IF"] = _evals_df[_static_mask].groupby("model")["score"].mean()
if _has_dynamic:
    _summary_cols["Dynamic avg IF"] = _evals_df[~_static_mask].groupby("model")["score"].mean()
_summary_stats = pd.DataFrame(_summary_cols).reset_index() if _summary_cols else None

# --- Behavior boundaries (80% threshold) ---
# Note: NOT averaged over instruction templates - filter by instruction instead

BOUNDARY_THRESHOLD = 0.80
_boundary_df = _evals_df[["model", "condition", "instruction", "n_turns", "score"]].copy()
_boundary_df["_n_sort"] = _boundary_df["n_turns"].astype(int)
_boundary_df = _boundary_df.sort_values(["model", "condition", "instruction", "_n_sort"])
_boundary_df = _boundary_df.drop(columns=["_n_sort"])


def _boundary_stats(group):
    scores = group["score"]
    n_ints = group["n_turns"].astype(int)
    n_t = int((scores >= BOUNDARY_THRESHOLD).sum())
    n_p = int((scores <= (1 - BOUNDARY_THRESHOLD)).sum())
    p_mask = (scores <= (1 - BOUNDARY_THRESHOLD)) & (n_ints > 1)
    n_p_start = int(n_ints[p_mask].min()) if p_mask.any() else None
    return pd.Series({"N_T_width": n_t, "N_P_width": n_p, "N_P_start": n_p_start})


_boundaries = (
    _boundary_df.groupby(["model", "condition", "instruction"])
    .apply(_boundary_stats)
    .reset_index()
)
_boundary_path = str(_out_dir / "_boundaries.parquet")
_boundaries.to_parquet(_boundary_path, index=False)

# --- Behavioral consistency (stderr) derived dataset ---
# Keep instruction column for filtering (no averaging over templates)

_n_models_all = _evals_df["model"].nunique()
_stderr_height = max(300, _n_models_all * 30 + 80)
_stderr_df = _evals_df[["model", "condition", "instruction", "n_turns", "score_stderr"]].copy()
_stderr_df["score_stderr"] = _stderr_df["score_stderr"].round(3)
_stderr_df["_n_sort"] = _stderr_df["n_turns"].astype(int)
_stderr_df = _stderr_df.sort_values(["model", "condition", "instruction", "_n_sort"])
_stderr_df = _stderr_df.drop(columns=["_n_sort"])
_stderr_path = str(_out_dir / "_stderr.parquet")
_stderr_df.to_parquet(_stderr_path, index=False)

# --- Bullet graph data: neutral baseline cross-joined with non-neutral condition_pairs ---
_nonneutral_df = _evals_df[_evals_df["condition"] != "neutral"].copy()
_nonneutral_path = str(_out_dir / "_nonneutral.parquet")
_nonneutral_df.to_parquet(_nonneutral_path, index=False)

# Neutral scores need to appear in every condition facet row.
# Cross-join neutral rows with all non-neutral condition values.
_neutral_df = _evals_df[_evals_df["condition"] == "neutral"].copy()
_nn_conditions = _nonneutral_df["condition"].unique()
_baseline_rows = []
for cond in _nn_conditions:
    _tmp = _neutral_df.copy()
    _tmp["condition"] = cond
    _baseline_rows.append(_tmp)
_baseline_df = pd.concat(_baseline_rows, ignore_index=True)
_baseline_path = str(_out_dir / "_neutral_baseline.parquet")
_baseline_df.to_parquet(_baseline_path, index=False)

# Paired bullet graph: only condition pairs with a true alignment axis
_paired_df = _nonneutral_df[_nonneutral_df["condition_pair"].isin(ALIGNMENT_AXIS_PAIRS)]
_aligned_df = _paired_df[_paired_df["instruction_aligned"] == True].copy()
_aligned_path = str(_out_dir / "_aligned.parquet")
_aligned_df.to_parquet(_aligned_path, index=False)

_misaligned_df = _paired_df[_paired_df["instruction_aligned"] == False].copy()
_misaligned_path = str(_out_dir / "_misaligned.parquet")
_misaligned_df.to_parquet(_misaligned_path, index=False)

# Height for bullet graphs: ~30px per (condition × model) row + margins
_n_models = _evals_df["model"].nunique()
_bullet_height = _nonneutral_df["condition"].nunique() * _n_models * 30 + 130
_paired_height = _aligned_df["condition_pair"].nunique() * _n_models * 30 + 130
```

## Overview

Protocol 1 tests whether models follow global instructions (output T) or succumb to local induction pressure (output P) as the number of hardcoded turns N increases.

## Labeled Line Chart {#sec-trajectory}

Each line represents a model's instruction-following rate. Text labels appear at the rightmost N value. Use the dropdowns to filter by condition, model, and instruction template.

```{python}
#| echo: false
line_data = Data.from_file(evals_path_resolved)
label_data = Data.from_file(_label_path)
sel = Selection.intersect()
```

:::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-3 .bg-light .p-3 style="position: sticky; top: 1rem; align-self: start;"}
#### Filters

```{python}
#| echo: false
select(line_data, label="Condition", column="condition", value="auto", target=sel, field="condition")
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
select(line_data, label="Model", column="model", value="auto", target=sel, field="model")
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
select(line_data, label="Instruction", column="instruction", value="auto", target=sel, field="instruction")
```
:::

::: {.g-col-9}
```{python}
#| echo: false
labeled_line_chart(
    line_data,
    label_data,
    selection=sel,
    title="Instruction Following Rate vs N",
    x_domain=n_domain,
    width=900,
)
```
:::

::::

## Summary Statistics {#sec-summary}

Mean instruction-following rate averaged over all N values and instruction templates.

**Static** conditions use exact string matching (neutral baseline, value alignment, factual alignment). **Dynamic** conditions involve stylistic or semantic transformation (token sets, language, persona, style, preference).

```{python}
#| echo: false
if _summary_stats is not None:
    _score_cols = [c for c in _summary_stats.columns if c != "model"]
    _fmt = {c: "{:.1%}" for c in _score_cols}
    display(_summary_stats.style.format(_fmt).hide(axis="index"))
else:
    print("No data available.")
```

## Bullet Graph: vs Neutral Baseline {#sec-bullet}

Each row is a model. The grey background bar shows the neutral condition's accuracy; the colored foreground bar shows the selected condition. Faceted horizontally by instruction template and vertically by condition. Use the dropdown to select N (number of hardcoded turns).

```{python}
#| echo: false
bullet_main = Data.from_file(_nonneutral_path)
bullet_baseline = Data.from_file(_baseline_path)
bullet_sel = Selection.intersect()
```

:::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-3 .bg-light .p-3 style="position: sticky; top: 1rem; align-self: start;"}
#### N Turns

```{python}
#| echo: false
select(bullet_main, label="N (hardcoded turns)", column="n_turns", options=n_domain, value=n_domain[0], target=bullet_sel, field="n_turns")
```
:::

::: {.g-col-9}
```{python}
#| echo: false
bullet_graph(
    bullet_main,
    bullet_baseline,
    selection=bullet_sel,
    width=900,
    height=_bullet_height,
)
```
:::

::::

## Bullet Graph: Aligned vs Misaligned {#sec-paired-bullet}

Each row is a condition pair. Within each pair, the two conditions share the same pattern P but flip which response is the instruction target T:

- **Colored bar (aligned)**: instruction asks for the value/truth-consistent response (e.g., T="I love cats", P="I hate cats"). Following the instruction also agrees with the model's values.
- **Grey bar (misaligned)**: instruction asks for the counter-conventional response (e.g., T="I hate cats", P="I love cats"). Following the instruction requires overriding both induction *and* the model's values.

```{python}
#| echo: false
aligned_data = Data.from_file(_aligned_path)
misaligned_data = Data.from_file(_misaligned_path)
paired_sel = Selection.intersect()
```

:::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-3 .bg-light .p-3 style="position: sticky; top: 1rem; align-self: start;"}
#### N Turns

```{python}
#| echo: false
select(aligned_data, label="N (hardcoded turns)", column="n_turns", options=n_domain, value=n_domain[0], target=paired_sel, field="n_turns")
```
:::

::: {.g-col-9}
```{python}
#| echo: false
paired_bullet_graph(
    aligned_data,
    misaligned_data,
    selection=paired_sel,
    width=900,
    height=_paired_height,
)
```
:::

::::

## Behavior Boundaries {#sec-boundaries}

For each model × condition, N values are classified using an 80% threshold on instruction-following rate:

- **T-region** (IF ≥ 80%): model reliably follows the instruction.
- **P-region** (IF ≤ 20%): induction dominates.
- **Mixed** (20%–80%): neither fully dominates.

Note that T-width and P-width are not complementary — a wide gap between them indicates a mixed region.

### instruction_hint

**N_T_width** — number of N values with IF ≥ 80%. Higher = more persistent instruction following.

```{python}
#| echo: false
_instr_df = _boundaries[_boundaries["instruction"] == "instruction_hint"]
_nt_pivot = _instr_df.pivot(index="condition", columns="model", values="N_T_width")
_nt_pivot.columns.name = None
_nt_pivot.fillna(0).astype(int)
```

**N_P_width** — number of N values with IF ≤ 20%. Higher = more induction-dominated behavior.

```{python}
#| echo: false
_np_pivot = _instr_df.pivot(index="condition", columns="model", values="N_P_width")
_np_pivot.columns.name = None
_np_pivot.fillna(0).astype(int)
```

**N_P_start** — first N > 1 where IF ≤ 20%. `—` = P never dominates. Lower = earlier collapse.

```{python}
#| echo: false
_nps_pivot = _instr_df.pivot(index="condition", columns="model", values="N_P_start")
_nps_pivot.columns.name = None
_nps_pivot.map(lambda x: "—" if pd.isna(x) else str(int(x)))
```

### instruction_no_hint

**N_T_width** — number of N values with IF ≥ 80%. Higher = more persistent instruction following.

```{python}
#| echo: false
_instr_df = _boundaries[_boundaries["instruction"] == "instruction_no_hint"]
_nt_pivot = _instr_df.pivot(index="condition", columns="model", values="N_T_width")
_nt_pivot.columns.name = None
_nt_pivot.fillna(0).astype(int)
```

**N_P_width** — number of N values with IF ≤ 20%. Higher = more induction-dominated behavior.

```{python}
#| echo: false
_np_pivot = _instr_df.pivot(index="condition", columns="model", values="N_P_width")
_np_pivot.columns.name = None
_np_pivot.fillna(0).astype(int)
```

**N_P_start** — first N > 1 where IF ≤ 20%. `—` = P never dominates. Lower = earlier collapse.

```{python}
#| echo: false
_nps_pivot = _instr_df.pivot(index="condition", columns="model", values="N_P_start")
_nps_pivot.columns.name = None
_nps_pivot.map(lambda x: "—" if pd.isna(x) else str(int(x)))
```

## Behavioral Consistency {#sec-consistency}

Standard error of the instruction-following score at each N × condition. **Lower values indicate stable behavior** (reliably T or reliably P across trials); higher values indicate mixed, inconsistent responses. Use the dropdowns to filter by condition and instruction template.

```{python}
#| echo: false
stderr_data = Data.from_file(_stderr_path)
stderr_sel = Selection.intersect()
```

:::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-3 .bg-light .p-3 style="position: sticky; top: 1rem; align-self: start;"}
#### Filters

```{python}
#| echo: false
select(stderr_data, label="Condition", column="condition", value="auto", target=stderr_sel, field="condition")
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
select(stderr_data, label="Instruction", column="instruction", value="auto", target=stderr_sel, field="instruction")
```
:::

::: {.g-col-9}
```{python}
#| echo: false
overview_heatmap(
    stderr_data,
    x="n_turns",
    y="model",
    fill_col="score_stderr",
    selection=stderr_sel,
    title="Behavioral Consistency — Standard Error of IF Rate",
    color_scheme="reds",
    show_text=True,
    text_color="black",
    x_domain=n_domain,
    width=900,
    height=_stderr_height,
)
```
:::

::::
